prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `b7955b3c`.
codex_response:
Implemented the `b7955b3c` ARC2 package in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b7955b3c/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b7955b3c/verifier.py), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b7955b3c/generator.py). The verifier treats each visible non-`8` color as one rectangle, infers rectangle paint order from overlap witnesses on the observed cells, then replaces `8` with the bottom color and repaints the rectangles in topological order. The summary hint was consistent with the official examples, so I used it and did not add `notes.md`. No new primitive was needed.

The generator constructs the clean image directly as a full-grid background plus overlapping colored rectangles, then injects protected `8` corruption so each color keeps its bounding box and overlap evidence. That keeps the samples verifier-stable while matching the task family visually; `preview_01.png` looked on-family as layered rectangle restorations with scattered/clumped `8` damage. Validation passed: official examples `5/5`, generated examples `1000/1000`. Artifacts are [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/b7955b3c/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/b7955b3c/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/b7955b3c/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/b7955b3c/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/b7955b3c/previews/preview_03.png).
